meta_title: Impact Doors in Boca Raton, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Boca Raton, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind rated, licensed & insured. Get a free quote today!
h1: Boca Raton's Trusted Impact Door Specialists — Built for Beauty, Engineered for Storms
intro:
Boca Raton is one of South Florida's most desirable addresses, and with average home values hovering around $650,000, protecting your investment is more than a seasonal concern — it's a year-round responsibility. From the oceanfront estates of East Boca to the sprawling estates in Broken Sound and Mission Bay, homeowners across this city understand that hurricane season doesn't discriminate by zip code. Whether your property sits in 33432 along the Intracoastal or deep into West Boca's 33498 communities, every entry point of your home needs to perform under pressure — literally.

Impact doors represent one of the smartest upgrades a Boca Raton homeowner can make. Positioned just 2.5 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Boca Raton sits squarely in a 170mph wind zone, meaning the doors protecting your home must meet stringent Florida Building Code standards for hurricane resistance. While Boca Raton falls just outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that covers Miami-Dade and portions of Broward — the city still requires hurricane-rated products on all new door installations and replacements. Older homes, which make up roughly 45% of Boca Raton's housing stock and were built before the post-Hurricane Andrew code reforms of 1994, are especially vulnerable and represent the most urgent candidates for door replacement.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've spent years helping families across Boca Del Mar, Town Center, and the communities surrounding Mizner Park and FAU's campus make the transition from outdated, single-pane or hollow-core doors to fully code-compliant, elegantly designed impact doors. Our product lineup features entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors from ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — brands that combine cutting-edge impact resistance with the architectural aesthetics that Boca Raton homes deserve. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just buying a door — you're buying peace of mind backed by The Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Boca Raton Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Upgrades
section_why.body:
There's no shortage of contractors in South Florida willing to sell you a door. What sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our unwavering commitment to doing the job right — every time, on every home, in every neighborhood. With 60+ years of combined installation experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our team has seen every home type, every structural challenge, and every code requirement this region has to offer. That expertise matters when you're investing in impact doors for a $650,000 Boca Raton property.

One of our most important differentiators is our 100% in-house installation model. We never subcontract our crews. Every technician who shows up to your home in Broken Sound or Mission Bay is a trained, vetted Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee who understands our quality standards inside and out. This means consistent workmanship, direct accountability, and a finished product that passes inspection on the first visit — not after repeated callbacks. We've completed impact door and window installations on 500+ homes throughout South Florida, and our A+ BBB rating reflects the trust homeowners place in us.

We also understand Boca Raton's specific permitting environment. Working with the City of Boca Raton Building Division requires attention to detail — submitting the correct product approvals, ensuring NOA (Notice of Acceptability) documentation is current, and coordinating inspections efficiently. Our project managers handle all of this on your behalf, so you never have to navigate city hall alone. We know which inspectors to call, what documentation they require, and how to keep your project on schedule.

Finally, we believe premium home protection should be financially accessible. That's why we offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available. For Boca Raton homeowners looking to protect their equity while managing cash flow, our financing programs make it possible to upgrade today without waiting for the next storm season to remind you why it matters.

section_history.h2: Boca Raton's Storm History Makes Impact Doors a Non-Negotiable Investment
section_history.body:
Boca Raton has experienced the full fury of South Florida's hurricane history. Since 1990, the city has been affected by at least 8 named storms — a sobering statistic for any homeowner relying on standard doors for protection. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was the watershed event that revealed just how catastrophically underprepared South Florida homes were, prompting a complete overhaul of the state's building codes. Homes in Boca Del Mar and East Boca built before that landmark storm — which represents nearly half of the city's housing stock — were constructed to standards that simply don't hold up against modern storm science.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought widespread damage across Palm Beach County, with sustained winds and storm surge that exposed the weakness of standard sliding glass doors and hollow-core entry doors throughout the area. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly up the Florida peninsula and subjected Boca Raton to prolonged tropical storm and Category 1 conditions. Downed trees, flooded streets near Red Reef Park, and wind-driven debris caused significant structural damage — much of it entering homes through compromised door systems. Hurricane Dorian in 2019 served as another jarring reminder, skirting close enough to the coast to produce damaging wind gusts even as it stayed just offshore.

The pattern is clear: Boca Raton is not immune to storm damage, and the doors that protect your home are often the first point of failure. Impact doors are engineered specifically to resist the projectile impact of wind-borne debris — the broken tree limb, the airborne lawn furniture, the displaced roof tile — while simultaneously maintaining a weathertight seal against rain infiltration. For homeowners in communities like Town Center and West Boca who lived through any of these events, impact doors aren't a luxury upgrade. They're the responsible choice.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Boca Raton and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Understanding the regulatory landscape in Boca Raton is essential before any door replacement project begins. Florida operates under one of the most rigorous building codes in the nation — and for good reason. The Florida Building Code (FBC) requires that all exterior doors in Palm Beach County meet specific large-missile impact standards, particularly for homes within 1 mile of the coast or in designated wind zones. Boca Raton's 170mph wind zone classification means that every door installed must be rated and tested to withstand those conditions, using products that carry current Florida Product Approvals (FPA) or Miami-Dade Notices of Acceptability (NOA).

While Boca Raton is not classified as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — which is the strictest designation under the FBC and applies primarily to Miami-Dade County and parts of Broward — the city still requires hurricane-rated opening protection on all new installations and permitted replacements. This means that standard doors purchased at a big-box retailer will not pass inspection. Only tested and approved impact-rated products installed by licensed contractors will receive sign-off from the City of Boca Raton Building Division.

The permitting process through the City of Boca Raton Building Division requires submittal of product approval documents, a signed and sealed installation drawing, and scheduling of a field inspection upon completion. Our team handles every step of this process for you — from permit application to final inspection approval. We stay current on all Palm Beach County amendments to the FBC, and our products are always sourced with full documentation in hand. This compliance-first approach protects your home, your warranty, and your homeowner's insurance policy — all of which can be voided by unpermitted work.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Performance Engineered for Boca Raton Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors exclusively from two brands we trust with our own families' homes: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. These manufacturers have earned their reputations by producing products that consistently pass Florida's rigorous large-missile impact testing — not just on paper, but in the field, storm after storm. When we recommend these brands to homeowners in East Boca or Boca Del Mar, it's because we've watched them perform under real conditions and stand behind their warranties without fine print excuses.

ESW's impact door lineup includes beautifully engineered entry doors, French doors, and sliding glass door systems that are rated for 170mph winds and beyond. Their multi-point locking systems are a standout feature — rather than securing at a single point like a traditional deadbolt, these systems engage multiple lock points along the door frame simultaneously, creating a fortress-like seal that resists both wind pressure and forced entry. For Boca Raton homeowners who value both security and aesthetics, ESW offers a wide selection of finishes, glass configurations, and frame profiles that complement high-end architectural styles from Mediterranean to contemporary.

CWI rounds out our product offering with equally impressive impact door systems, particularly in the sliding glass door category — a critical upgrade for Boca Raton homes where large glass panels face east toward prevailing storm tracks. CWI sliding glass doors feature reinforced frames, laminated impact glass, and smooth-glide hardware systems that maintain their function even after years of salt air exposure along the coast. All of our ESW and CWI products carry the required Florida Product Approvals and are backed by manufacturer warranties. Combined with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installation guarantee, you're protected from every angle.

section_cost.h2: What Boca Raton Homeowners Can Expect to Invest in Impact Door Upgrades
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact doors varies based on door type, size, glass configuration, and the number of openings being replaced. For a typical Boca Raton home — valued at approximately $650,000 and featuring a mix of entry doors, a sliding glass door, and perhaps a set of French doors leading to a lanai — a full impact door package can range from approximately $4,000 to $15,000 or more depending on scope and product selection. Entry door replacements tend to be more straightforward in cost, while large-format sliding glass systems and custom French door configurations command a higher investment reflecting their size and engineering complexity.

The good news is that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options specifically designed for homeowners who want to protect their investment without disrupting their household budget. Our financing starts at just $87 per month with $0 down available to qualified applicants. For a $650,000 property in Broken Sound or Mission Bay, that monthly investment is modest compared to the cost of storm damage repairs — a single failed door during a hurricane can result in $20,000 to $50,000 in interior damage from wind, rain, and debris intrusion.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors also add measurable resale value to Boca Raton properties. Buyers in this market actively seek homes with hurricane protection in place, and a fully upgraded, code-compliant door system is a compelling selling point in a competitive real estate market. Add in the insurance savings discussed in the next section, and the real cost of impact doors — when amortized over years of savings and enhanced home value — is far lower than the sticker price suggests.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Boca Raton Impact Door Installations
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediately tangible benefits of upgrading to impact doors in Boca Raton is the potential for significant homeowner's insurance premium reductions. Florida's property insurance market is among the most expensive in the nation, and Palm Beach County homeowners feel that pressure acutely. Major carriers operating in this market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — offer premium discounts for homes with verified hurricane-rated opening protection, including impact doors and windows.

The mechanism for capturing these savings is the wind mitigation inspection and report. After your impact doors are installed and permitted through the City of Boca Raton Building Division, a licensed wind mitigation inspector evaluates your home's storm resistance features and submits a report to your insurance carrier. Impact-rated exterior doors — particularly those with multi-point locking systems and laminated impact glass — qualify for Opening Protection credits under Florida's wind mitigation rating system. Depending on the percentage of openings protected and the rating of the products installed, Boca Raton homeowners have reported annual insurance savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars per year.

It's worth noting that for homes built before 1994 — which represent nearly half of Boca Raton's housing stock — insurance carriers may currently be applying surcharges or coverage limitations due to the lack of hurricane-rated opening protection. Upgrading to ESW or CWI impact doors can not only qualify you for new discounts but may also remove existing premium penalties. We strongly recommend requesting a wind mitigation inspection within 90 days of project completion and submitting the report to your insurance agent promptly to begin capturing those savings as soon as your policy renews.

faqs.q: Are impact doors required in Boca Raton, or are shutters an acceptable alternative?
faqs.a: Both impact doors and hurricane shutters are recognized under the Florida Building Code as acceptable forms of opening protection in Boca Raton. However, impact doors offer a significant practical advantage — they provide permanent, always-in-place protection without the need to deploy shutters before every storm. For Boca Raton homeowners who travel frequently or own second homes in communities like Broken Sound or Mission Bay, impact doors eliminate the risk of being caught unprepared when a hurricane approaches with short notice.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Boca Raton, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door pricing in Boca Raton typically ranges from $4,000 to $15,000+ depending on the number, type, and size of doors being replaced. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified homeowners. We provide free, no-obligation quotes so you can understand the full scope of your project investment before making any commitment. Our financing is designed to make upgrades accessible regardless of where you are in your budget planning.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Boca Raton?
faqs.a: Yes. The City of Boca Raton Building Division requires a permit for any exterior door replacement, including upgrades to impact-rated products. This process involves submitting Florida Product Approval documentation, installation drawings, and scheduling a final inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application through inspection sign-off — ensuring your installation is fully code-compliant and your homeowner's insurance policy remains valid and unencumbered by unpermitted work.

faqs.q: How long does it take to install impact doors on a typical Boca Raton home?
faqs.a: The installation itself is typically completed in one to two days for a standard package of entry, sliding glass, and French doors on a Boca Raton home. Lead time from signed contract to installation date generally ranges from four to eight weeks, depending on product availability and permit processing through the City of Boca Raton Building Division. Our project managers keep you informed throughout the timeline and will coordinate scheduling to minimize disruption to your household routine.

faqs.q: Will impact doors actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Boca Raton?
faqs.a: Yes — in most cases, they will. After installation and permitting, a wind mitigation inspection will document your impact-rated opening protection, and that report submitted to your insurance carrier can qualify your home for significant premium reductions under Florida's wind mitigation discount program. Boca Raton homeowners with properly documented impact doors have reported annual savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars depending on their carrier, coverage level, and the percentage of protected openings on the home.
